title: Submodule Locus of 7B+ Coupling
hypothesis: The 7B+ surgery x jitter coupling (confirmed in exp-014/015/016 but mechanism-unresolved) is carried by a specific transformer submodule pathway: most likely the attention/residual branch rather than the MLP branch. Exp-016 ruled out layer localization, training regularization, and geometric prediction.
value_to_investors: Determines which transformer submodule carries the coupling. If attention-localized, deployment perturbation testing can focus on attention outputs. If MLP-localized, MLP monitoring suffices. If residual integration, full-block testing required. Directly informs testing protocol efficiency.
value_to_researchers: First submodule decomposition of the 7B+ surgery-jitter interaction. Tests three pathway hypotheses (attention, MLP, residual integration) with 30,720 factorial trials, wild-cluster bootstrap (exact enumeration), and branch diagnostic measurements. Connects perturbation effects to specific computational pathways.
identification_strategy: Same 2x2 factorial as exp-014/015/016 (surgery=0.08, jitter=0.08) applied at 3 hook targets (full block output, self_attn output, mlp output) x 2 layer positions [0.50, 0.80]. 4 models (3 base_transformer, 1 reasoning_tuned). Phase 1: 30,720 factorial trials. Phase 2: clean branch diagnostics (norms, cosine alignment). Interaction computed per model x layer x target. Wild-cluster bootstrap for p-values. Model-clustered bootstrap for CIs.
falsification_tests: If self_attn shows no significant interaction (CI includes zero), H1 (attention path) is falsified
If mlp shows no significant interaction (CI includes zero), H2 (MLP path) is falsified
If both branches show significant interaction individually, clean separation is not possible
If calibration-holdout gap > 5pp, prompt-level overfitting is present
success_criteria: All 4 models complete (all cached, no downloads needed)
Clear verdict: ATTENTION_PATH_COUPLING, MLP_PATH_COUPLING, RESIDUAL_STREAM_INTEGRATION, GENERIC_BLOCK_COUPLING, or STOP_MECHANISM_CHASING
At least one target shows distinct behavior from others
Branch diagnostics collected at all 8 model-layer positions

data_lineage.source: HuggingFace Hub models (all pre-cached from exp-014/015/016)
data_lineage.provenance: 4 models at 7B+ (3 base transformer, 1 reasoning-tuned transformer), NF4 quantized via bitsandbytes on RTX 5090 Laptop (23.9GB VRAM). All models cached locally from prior experiments.
data_lineage.transformations: Phase 1: Forward pass with hook-based hidden-state perturbation at 3 submodule targets (block, self_attn, mlp) x 2 layer positions, greedy decoding (max_new_tokens=10), strict answer parsing. Phase 2: Clean forward pass with branch capture hooks measuring output norms and cosine alignment.

out_of_sample_validation_plan: 32 calibration prompts used for analysis tuning; 32 holdout prompts reserved for final validation. Interaction computed on both splits per target with divergence check.
uncertainty_methods: Wild-cluster bootstrap (exact enumeration of 2^4=16 sign combinations)
Cal vs holdout split comparison per target
robustness_checks: Mixed-effects logistic regression with target x interaction terms
